purpose: Bootstrap module for the node group; installs current LTS Node.js from the NodeSource apt repo and adds pnpm and tsx as the only two global npm tools via the user-prefix npm helper.
invariants: Node.js is always installed from NodeSource (not Debian apt) to get current LTS — NODE_MAJOR=22 is hardcoded
node_runtime is fully guarded: if node is already on PATH, the NodeSource repo is never added and nodejs is never reinstalled
Only pnpm and tsx are installed globally — all other JS tooling is project-local in node_modules
pnpm and tsx land in ~/.local/bin via npm_global (user prefix), requiring no sudo and no extra PATH entry
gotchas: NODE_MAJOR is a module-level variable set to 22 — bumping it requires editing this file and re-running the node group
The NodeSource setup script is piped directly to sudo bash — verify the URL before use in airgapped environments
npm is bundled with Node.js (nodesource); its manifest entry is recorded separately from the nodejs entry
npm_global has a side effect of setting a global npm prefix (~/.local) on the first call — this is intentional and safe but affects npm behavior globally

key_functions.name: node_runtime
key_functions.summary: Adds the NodeSource apt repo for Node 22 and installs nodejs — skipped entirely if node is already on PATH

key_functions.name: node_global_tools
key_functions.summary: Installs pnpm and tsx into the user npm prefix (~/.local/bin) via npm_global — no sudo required

key_functions.name: node_record_manifest
key_functions.summary: Upserts manifest entries for nodejs, npm, pnpm, and tsx if their binaries are present
